brief_justification: Correctly identifies (B) with valid causal chain reasoning and empirical grounding in the 5% vs 40% intervention gap; traces three levels deep — intrinsic trait → volume → attention dilution → per-post metric — and connects intervention failure to causal model; preamble explicitly flags the total-likes vs likes-per-post conflation risk, showing targeted bias awareness before reasoning; uses the intervention result as an empirical sanity check and eliminates alternatives systematically, though no formal re-derivation from a different angle; acknowledges the conditional framing ('if the reverse-causal model is correct') but does not assign confidence levels or identify what evidence would falsify the reverse model; all three alternative options are evaluated with model-specific rejection reasons, not just dismissed; every conclusion is traceable — causal chain, alternative eliminations, and empirical corroboration are all explicit; avoids the forward-causality-only trap (the negative gate pattern) and explicitly names the reverse direction, showing clear awareness of why the PM interpretation was wrong; none of the suppressed failure modes (linear causation, feedback loop blindness, linear extrapolation) appear in the response, and at least one is explicitly addressed; reasoning proceeds largely linearly rather than following the topology's gate-and-branch structure (S1→classify→G1→G2 etc.), with no visible loop-detection or reclassification steps mapped from the DAG.
